description: county in Georgia, United States
description_wiki: Monroe County is a county located in the central portion of the U.S. state of Georgia.

description: severe economic crisis in the USA in the 1930s
description_wiki: In the United States, the Great Depression is commonly (though not universally) dated to the Wall Street crash of October 1929.

description: military unit
description_wiki: The Georgia National Guard is the National Guard of the U.S. state of Georgia, and consists of the Georgia Army National Guard and the Georgia Air National Guard.

description: maritime land force service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Marine Corps (USMC), also referred to as the United States Marines or simply the Marines, is the naval infantry service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: US Marine Corps base near Beaufort, South Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Marine Corps Recruit Depot Parris Island (often abbreviated as MCRD PI) is an 8,095-acre (3,276 ha) military installation located within Port Royal, South Carolina, approximately 5 miles (8.0 km) south of Beaufort, the community that is typically associated with the installation.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: South Carolina (  KARR-ə-LY-nə) is a state in the Southeastern, South Atlantic and Deep South regions of the United States.

description: independent city in Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Norfolk (locally   NOR-fək) is an independent city in the U.S. commonwealth of Virginia.

description: President of Cuba, 1940–1944; dictator, 1952-1959 (1901-1973)
description_wiki: Fulgencio Batista y Zaldívar (born Rubén Zaldívar; January 16, 1901 – August 6, 1973) was a Cuban military officer, political leader, and dictator who played a dominant role in Cuban politics from his initial rise to power in the 1930s until his overthrow in the Cuban Revolution in 1959.

description: sovereign state situated on an island in the Caribbean Sea
description_wiki: Cuba, officially the Republic of Cuba, is an island country in the Caribbean.

USS Romain was a Auxiliary Wooden Schooner in service with the United States Navy briefly during World War II. Built in 1937 as Mérida by Symonette Ship Building Co., she was acquired by the U.S. Navy on 18 August 1942.

description: infantry battalion of the United States Marine Corps
description_wiki: 1st Battalion, 5th Marines (1/5) is an infantry battalion in the United States Marine Corps based out of Marine Corps Base Camp Pendleton, California consisting of approximately 800 Marines and sailors.

description: military unit
description_wiki: 2nd Battalion 5th Marines (2/5 or "Two Five") is an infantry battalion in the United States Marine Corps consisting of approximately 800 marines and sailors.

description: capital and largest city of Cuba
description_wiki: Havana (, US also ; Spanish: La Habana [la‿aˈβana] ) is the capital and largest city of Cuba.

description: island-municipality in Puerto Rico
description_wiki: Vieques ( ; Spanish pronunciation: [ˈbjekes], locally [ˈbjekeʔ]), officially the Isla de Vieques, is an island and municipality of Puerto Rico, comprising the Spanish Virgin Islands together with Culebra.

surface_forms: Culebra was the name of the island
description: island-municipality in Puerto Rico
description_wiki: Culebra, officially Isla de Culebra (Spanish pronunciation: [kuˈleβɾa], "Snake Island"), is a small archipelago and island organized as a municipality of Puerto Rico.

description: state-supported military college in Lexington, Virginia, USA
description_wiki: The Virginia Military Institute (VMI) is a public senior military college in Lexington, Virginia, United States.

aliases: Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University
description: public university with the main campus in Blacksburg, Virginia
description_wiki: The Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University, commonly referred to as Virginia Tech (VT), is a public land-grant research university with its main campus in Blacksburg, Virginia, United States.

description: Louisiana National Guard installation near Pineville, LA
description_wiki: Camp Beauregard is a Louisiana National Guard installation located northeast of Pineville, Louisiana, primarily in Rapides Parish, but also extending northward into Grant Parish.

description: largest city in Pennsylvania, United States
description_wiki: Philadelphia (  FIL-ə-DEL-fee-ə), colloquially referred to as Philly, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Pennsylvania, and the sixth-most populous city in the United States, with a Census-estimated population of 1,574,281 in July 2025.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Ohio (  oh-HY-oh) is a state in the Midwestern region of the United States.

description: United States Navy shipyard in Virginia
description_wiki: The Norfolk Naval Shipyard, often called the Norfolk Navy Yard and abbreviated as NNSY, is a U.S. Navy facility in Portsmouth, Virginia.

description: 1936 Brooklyn-class light cruiser
description_wiki: USS Brooklyn (CL-40) was a light cruiser, the lead ship of her class of nine, and the third United States Navy ship to bear its name.

description: city in and county seat of Galveston County, Texas, United States; port and coastal city
description_wiki: Galveston ( GAL-vis-tən) is a resort city and port on the Gulf Coast of the U.S. state of Texas.

description: largest city of the state of Louisiana, United States
description_wiki: New Orleans (commonly known as NOLA or  The Big Easy, among other nicknames) is a consolidated city-parish located along the Mississippi River in the U.S. state of Louisiana.

aliases: United States Naval Station Guantanamo Bay
description: military base of the United States Navy
description_wiki: Guantanamo Bay Naval Base (Spanish: Base Naval de la Bahía de Guantánamo), officially known as Naval Station Guantanamo Bay or NSGB, (also called GTMO, pronounced Gitmo  GIT-moh as jargon by the U.S. military) is a United States military base located on 45 square miles (117 km2) of land and water on the shore of Guantánamo Bay at the southeastern end of Cuba.

description: country in the Caribbean Sea
description_wiki: Haiti, officially the Republic of Haiti, is a country in the Caribbean on the island of Hispaniola in both Caribbean Sea and North Atlantic Ocean, east of Cuba and Jamaica and south of the Bahamas.

aliases: Virgin Islands of the United States
description: unincorporated territory of the United States of America
description_wiki: The United States Virgin Islands, officially the Virgin Islands of the United States, are a Caribbean insular  territory of the United States.

description: sovereign state in Central America
description_wiki: Panama, officially the Republic of Panama, is a country located at the southern end of Central America in North America, bordering South America.

description: island sovereign state in the Caribbean
description_wiki: Trinidad and Tobago, officially the Republic of Trinidad and Tobago, is the southernmost island country in the Caribbean, comprising the main islands of Trinidad and Tobago, along with several smaller islets.

description: dictator of Germany from 1933 to 1945, main instigator of World War II (1889–1945)
description_wiki: Adolf Hitler (20 April 1889 – 30 April 1945) was an Austrian-born  German politician who was the dictator of Germany during the Nazi era from 1933 until his suicide in 1945.

description: electric utility in Atlanta, Georgia, United States
description_wiki: Georgia Power is an electric utility headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, United States.

description: US Marine Corps base in Jacksonville, North Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Marine Corps Base Camp Lejeune ( lə-ZHURN or  lə-ZHOON) is a 246-square-mile (640 km2) United States military training facility in Jacksonville, North Carolina.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: North Carolina (  KAYR-ə-LIE-nə) is a state in the Southeastern and South Atlantic regions of the United States.

description: 1945 major battle of the Pacific War
description_wiki: The Battle of Okinawa (Japanese: 沖縄戦, Hepburn: Okinawa-sen), codenamed Operation Iceberg, was a major battle of the Pacific War fought on the island of Okinawa by the United States Army and United States Marine Corps forces against the Imperial Japanese Army.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Okinawa (Japanese: 沖縄, Hepburn: Okinawa) most commonly refers to:

description: maritime branch of the United States military
description_wiki: The United States Navy (USN) is the maritime service branch of the United States Armed Forces and is designated as the navy of the United States in the Constitution.

description: District of Phu Tho, Vietnam
aerial service branch of the Chinese People's Liberation Army
description_wiki: The People's Liberation Army Air Force, also referred to as the Chinese Air Force (中国空军) or the People's Air Force (人民空军), is the primary aerial warfare service of the People's Liberation Army.

description: founding and ruling party of the People's Republic of China
description_wiki: The Communist Party of China (CPC), commonly known as the Chinese Communist Party (CCP), is the founding and ruling party of the People's Republic of China (PRC).

description: capital city of China
description_wiki: Beijing, previously romanized as Peking, is the capital city of China.

description: prefecture-level city in Shandong, China
description_wiki: Qingdao, archaically known as Tsingtao, is a prefecture-level city in the eastern Shandong Province of China.
